CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, x FLOAT, y FLOAT);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (1, 1.5, 2.5);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (2, NULL, 3.5);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (3, 4.0, NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (4, NULL, NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (5, 2.0, 2.0);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (6, 3.0, 2.0);
SELECT REGR_SXY(y, x) FROM test_data WHERE id > 0;
SELECT a.id, REGR_SXY(a.y, a.x) FROM test_data a JOIN test_data b ON a.id <> b.id GROUP BY a.id;
SELECT AVG(x) as avg_x, AVG(y) as avg_y, REGR_SXY(y, x) AS regression_sxy FROM test_data;
SELECT REGR_SXY(y, x) FROM (SELECT x, y FROM test_data WHERE x IS NOT NULL AND y IS NOT NULL) AS filtered_data;
SELECT REGR_SXY(y, x) FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE y > 1.0);
WITH valid_data AS (SELECT x, y FROM test_data WHERE x IS NOT NULL AND y IS NOT NULL) SELECT REGR_SXY(y, x) FROM valid_data;
